title: Perfumed Japanese Chicken Yakitori
description: A delicate and flavorful Japanese dish featuring free-range chicken, infused with floral notes from Japanese yoshino cherry blossoms and rendered fat for added richness.

ingredients.name: Yoshino cherry blossom honey
ingredients.notes: Available at Japanese markets or online

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together honey, sake, gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and black pepper to make the marinade.
In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces and marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Preheat a fondue pot over medium heat.
Add vegetable oil to the fondue pot and swirl to combine.
Remove chicken from marinade and add to the fondue pot in batches, if necessary. Cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Transfer cooked chicken to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the fondue pot, add sesame oil and swirl to combine.
Add beaten egg to the fondue pot and cook, stirring constantly, until the egg is cooked through and slightly caramelized, about 2-3 minutes.
Add cooked chicken back to the fondue pot and toss with egg mixture until combined.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped green onions.
Serve immediately, using fondue forks or skewers to dip cooked chicken into egg mixture.
